title: Daily Briefing — Monday, June 1
tldr: Play: run a capable open model on your own machine — free and private — with Ollama. Zero token cost, works offline, and your data never leaves your laptop. Perfect for the open-weight era.
The day's open-weight launch and IPO headlines were news; the durable, run-it-today move is getting a local model running below.

sections.h: Run it today: a private, free LLM on your laptop

blocks.sub: Use AI on confidential text without sending it to anyone
blocks.p: Payoff: no API bill, no rate limits, and full privacy for the work you can't paste into a cloud chatbot — contracts, client data, internal docs. Good enough for summarizing, drafting, classifying, and code help on a modern laptop.
blocks.doIt: Install Ollama (Mac/Windows/Linux, free). In a terminal run ollama run llama3.2 (or qwen2.5, gemma3) — it downloads once, then runs locally. Chat in the terminal, or point any app at the built-in local API at localhost:11434. Pick a small model (1–8B) for speed; a bigger one if you have the RAM.
blocks.note: Match model size to your RAM (an 8B model needs ~8GB free). For the hardest 10% of tasks, still reach for a frontier model — see the model router (May 30).

sources: Ollama — run open models locally
